articles.title: Lebanon and Israel officials to meet in US on Tuesday
articles.brief: Lebanon and Israel officials are scheduled to meet in the US on Tuesday. The meeting is the result of diplomatic efforts, with no further details provided on the agenda or expected outcomes.

articles.title: The five big sticking points in US-Iran talks
articles.brief: US and Iranian officials are set to meet in Pakistan on Saturday for peace talks, which will focus on several key issues. The five main sticking points in the talks include Iran's nuclear program, sanctions, terrorism, regional security, and the 2015 nuclear deal.

articles.title: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
articles.brief: A Palestinian man was shot dead during an attack by Israeli settlers on a village in the occupied West Bank. The incident occurred amid warnings from former security chiefs that "government-sponsored Jewish terrorism" was escalating in the region.

articles.title: Man accused of coercing wife into sex with 120 men goes on trial in Sweden
articles.brief: A 61-year-old man in Sweden has gone on trial, accused of coercing his wife into having sex with 120 men. He denies the allegations, claiming he did not use physical violence or drugs to force his wife into these encounters.

articles.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda locked in vicious 'civil war', say researchers
articles.brief: Researchers have observed a prolonged conflict among the Ngogo chimpanzees in Uganda, which has been ongoing for approximately eight years. The chimpanzees, once a close-knit community, have been engaged in a violent struggle, described as a "civil war" by the researchers.

articles.title: Dancer with MND performs on stage again through digital avatar
articles.brief: Breanna Olson, a dancer with motor neuron disease (MND), has performed on stage using a digital avatar that allows her to control movements through brain signals. The technology, which utilizes brainwave signals, has enabled Olson to re-establish the expression and connection she felt had been lost due to her condition.

articles.title: Meta pulls Facebook ads recruiting for social media addiction lawsuits
articles.brief:
I couldn't find any information about a recent landmark social media addiction trial in California that Meta lost. However, I can provide a general response based on the given information.

Meta has removed Facebook ads recruiting for social media addiction lawsuits.

articles.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
articles.brief: The White House has instructed staff to refrain from participating in prediction markets, citing concerns over potential conflicts of interest and the integrity of government operations. This directive is in response to the growing popularity of prediction markets, where users can place bets on various outcomes, including international conflicts.

articles.title: Mayor warns of London 'disinformation blizzard'
articles.brief: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan has warned of a "disinformation blizzard" targeting the city, claiming it is being portrayed as being in decline. According to Khan, London is being subjected to disinformation campaigns that aim to misrepresent the city's reality.

articles.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindles, prompting user outcry
articles.brief: Amazon will discontinue support for older Kindles released before 2013, affecting users' ability to download new e-books. This change means owners of these devices will no longer be able to access new e-book content from Amazon.

articles.title: Israeli strike on government building kills Lebanese officers
articles.brief: An Israeli air strike on a government building in Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, resulted in the deaths of at least 13 people, including Lebanese officers. The incident occurred during a period of heightened tensions between Israel and Lebanon.

articles.title: Brazil announces US partnership to intercept weapons, drug trafficking
articles.brief: Brazil's government has announced a partnership with the US to intercept weapons and drug trafficking. The partnership aims to combat illicit arms imports, with Brazil seizing 1,168 US-imported firearms in the last 12 months.
